title: SSR with Vue Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the Server-Side Rendering (SSR) benefits, hydration process, and universal frameworks like Nuxt.js.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the major difference between SSR and Client-Side Rendering (CSR)?
options: SSR is only for older browsers.
SSR generates the initial HTML on the server, while CSR generates it in the browser with JavaScript.
SSR only works once per day.

questions.question: Which and/or what is one of the biggest benefits of using SSR in a Vue application?
options: It makes the JavaScript files smaller.

questions.question: The process where Vue 'takes over' the server-rendered HTML and makes it interactive in the browser is called ________.
Which popular universal framework is commonly used to build professional SSR applications with Vue.js?
Does SSR improve 'Time to Content' on slow networks or devices?
options: Yes, as users see the content before JavaScript is fully loaded and executed.
